parts of the 00:41:32 United States since the 1940 s. 00:41:36 It involves injecting various substances into clouds to produce 00:41:42 precipitation rain snow or ice that falls to the ground 00:41:49 the process can cause ice to form and build up inside the clouds until it falls 00:41:58 some Western u.s. 00:41:59 States including Idaho and Colorado have increased cloud seeding in recent 00:42:06 years to help meet 00:42:08 a growing water demand. Until now however it has been difficult 00:42:15 for scientists to measure the effectiveness of the process 00:42:21 researchers have mainly used instruments to compare the amount of precipitation 00:42:28 from seeded clouds to similar clouds that were not seeded but such 00:42:34 studies have brought mixed results this is partly because many elements 00:42:41 influence natural precipitation which makes it hard to narrow down exact 00:42:47 cause and effect results the research operation over 00:42:54 western Idaho was called Snowie it involved shooting small amounts of 00:43:01 a chemical compound called silver iodide from an airplane into clouds 00:43:08 the chemical can cause water in the clouds to freeze into ice and then fall as snow 00:43:16 in the new experiments the scientists used radar equipment together with 00:43:22 precipitation measuring instruments to collect data the use of 00:43:29 airborne and ground based radar permitted the team to observe the entire process 00:43:36 and compare the side by side seated and unseeded areas the 00:43:43 n.c.a.r. 00:43:44 Said in 00:43:44 a statement in at least 3 cases the seeding measurably 00:43:51 boosted the snowfall across the targeted watershed the statements. 00:43:58 In some areas the cloud seeding created new snowfall where no natural snow existed 00:44:06 one cloud seeding flight resulted in precipitation that produced snowfall for about 00:44:13 67 minutes. The researchers estimated the 3 cases 00:44:19 described in the study produced 00:44:22 a combined precipitation total that could fill about $285.00 00:44:28 a lympics sized swimming pools The researchers also used 00:44:34 computer modeling to study precipitation measurements during simulated weather 00:44:39 activity the scientists say they hope to use the real results from the new 00:44:46 experiments to improve future computer modeling predictions 00:44:52 I'm Brian Len. 00:45:08 Welcome to the making of a nation American history in v.o.a. 00:45:14 Special English.
